title: Community Banking: Aligning Ideals with Practice
summary: A bank-regulation speech aimed at community bankers. Daly argues for right-sizing and tailoring regulation—weighing cumulative costs and benefits and matching rules to bank size, complexity, and risk—and notes she has no direct role in supervisory policy. It is entirely about regulatory and supervisory frameworks, with no read on the macro outlook, inflation, the labor market, or the stance of monetary policy. Non-policy for reaction-function purposes.

key_quotes.quote: The goal of regulation is not to remove all risk, but rather to ensure that banks manage it well.
key_quotes.context: Her core principle for bank regulation; a regulatory, not monetary-policy, statement.

key_quotes.quote: Carefully considered and appropriately tailored rules, that evolve as the economy does, can ensure that banks of all sizes take appropriate risks and continue to thrive.
key_quotes.context: Argument for tailoring supervisory frameworks to bank size and risk.
